title: Diodia dichotoma
intro: Diodia dichotoma, also known as Marsh Pennywort or Wild Pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America, characterized by its creeping stems and distinctive penny-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Diodia dichotoma contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support cardiovascular health.
taxonomy: Diodia dichotoma is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant is commonly known as the 'bushy button' or 'button weed'. Diodia dichotoma belongs to the genus Diodia, which is a small group of flowering plants. This species is often found in coastal areas and wetlands throughout its native range.
